commands.review_summary:
The PR changes WhatsApp Web monitor and login flows to preserve credentials on terminal disconnects, track logged-out auth in process, and move credential cleanup into explicit login, QR relink, reconnect, and logout recovery paths with focused tests.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +322. Total +330 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main deletes managed WhatsApp auth on logged-out and conflict terminal stops, while the PR preserves those files and moves cleanup into explicit recovery paths.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer PR should get human session-state and compatibility review; after the latest restart revalidation commit, there is no narrow automated repair to queue from this review.

target.title: fix(whatsapp): preserve auth on terminal disconnects
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve WhatsApp Web credentials on passive terminal disconnects instead of deleting auth from the monitor.
- Track same-process logged-out terminal state inside the WhatsApp plugin so explicit login/QR relink can clear stale creds deliberately.
- Clear that marker only after verified recovery paths: monitor reconnect, successful CLI login, successful QR login, or successful explicit logout cleanup.
- Keep logged-out login retry bounded to one cleanup/replacement and share the socket replacement path with the existing 408/515 restart handling.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.connection-and-logging.e2e.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/connection-controller.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auth-store.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/logout.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/login.coverage.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/login-qr.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel.logout.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- `git diff --check origin/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp passive monitor terminal disconnects no longer remove saved credentials, while explicit login/relink/logout flows still clean stale auth when they own the action.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw WhatsApp unit/e2e harness in the branch worktree.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.connection-and-logging.e2e.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/connection-controller.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auth-store.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/logout.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/login.coverage.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/login-qr.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel.logout.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: The focused run passed 1 e2e file with 26 tests and 6 WhatsApp extension files with 62 tests.

Observed result after fix: Passive logged-out monitor paths preserve auth and mark relink state; QR/CLI relink clears stale credentials deliberately; successful relink clears the marker so fresh credentials are not deleted later.

What was not tested: No live WhatsApp/Baileys QR scan, real-device logout, or conflict-session scenario was run.
target.author: mcaxtr
